runtime_algorithm.raw_vpk: interpolate points in dB over frequency

notes: Raw sweep used amp_code=0x40CC and PE43711 code=0x00.
20 MHz captured Vpp is expected to read about 4.56 Vpp, matching front-panel 4.6 Vpp.
20260607: Added high-frequency PE43711-only flatness trim. <=200 MHz keeps existing amplitude calibration; >200 MHz changes only PE43711 attenuation code after amp_code calculation.
2026-06-07: flattened RF 200 MHz-1 GHz calibration to 200 MHz PE43711 trim standard.
2026-06-07: Added RAM-waveform-only PE43711 trim from spectrum analyzer power table; target is 1.000 Vpp into 50 ohm (3.979 dBm), no external 20 dB attenuation.

amplitude_correction_model.description: Final correction_factor is interpolated over frequency and target_vpp; points are old_correction_factor*target_vpp/measured_vpp.

pe43711_frequency_trim_model.description: For RF >200 MHz, apply interpolated PE43711 attenuation delta only. Negative values reduce attenuation to compensate high-frequency flatness loss. Probe 20 dB affects absolute level but not relative delta.
pe43711_frequency_trim_model.notes: Flattened 200 MHz to 1 GHz to use the 200 MHz PE43711 trim standard.

ram_pe43711_frequency_trim_model.description: RAM-mode PE43711 frequency trim. atten_delta_db is added to the normal PE43711 attenuation; negative values reduce attenuation because the measured RAM-mode output was below 1 Vpp.
notes: Measured power is treated as direct spectrum-analyzer input power with no 20 dB external attenuator.
This model is selected only when HostApp output_mode == ram_waveform; NCO-only keeps pe43711_frequency_trim_model.
PE43711 can only realize the requested trim if the resulting absolute attenuation code remains within 0..127.
